seasonal_variants.description: The meadow grass goes gold. Seed heads lean over, ready to scatter, and the green slips quietly out of the stems.
The pond surface frosts over. Frogs sleep below in the soft mud, breathing through their skin until spring.
Monarchs return. The first orange wings drift north over the meadow, looking for milkweed to lay their eggs on.
Swallows skim the warm air, turning on a wingtip after insects too small to see from the ground.
